channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Karachi's ranking as one of the least liveable cities in 2026
item.description: Karachi has been ranked 170th out of 173 cities in the Economist Intelligence Unit’s Global Liveability Index 2026, placing it among the least liveable cities globally. The ranking highlights challeng...

item.title: US-Iran tensions escalate after strikes, India-Pakistan relations under spotlight, and global news updates
item.description: The article highlights major international developments on July 8, 2026. US forces launched airstrikes on Iran following attacks on commercial vessels in the Strait of Hormuz, escalating regional tens...

item.title: FIFA World Cup 2026 Quarter-Final Line-Up Revealed: Top Teams Battle in US
item.description: The FIFA World Cup 2026 has narrowed down to eight teams, with the quarter-final matches set to take place across the United States. The draw features high-profile clashes between France and Morocco, ...

item.title: First Nation calls for consultation on proposed Alberta-Sarnia oil pipeline amid environmental concerns
item.description: The chief of Aamjiwnaang First Nation, Janelle Nahmabin, has criticized the lack of consultation regarding a proposed oil pipeline between Alberta and Sarnia, Ontario. The project, announced by Albert...

item.title: A Comparative Review of Parmesan Cheeses from Australian Supermarkets
item.description: The article details an in-depth taste test of various parmesan cheeses available in Australian supermarkets. It evaluates brands such as Coles, Aldi, and specialty stores, highlighting their unique ch...

item.title: Nigerian Medical Student Killed in Russian Airstrike Near Graduation
item.description: Nnani Adaobi Marian, 23, a Nigerian medical student at Kharkiv National Medical University, died on July 5, 2026, after being injured in a Russian airstrike in Ukraine. She and her friend Fatima Husey...

item.title: Limpopo police seize R35000 worth of illicit cigarettes during targeted operation
item.description: A 25-year-old Zimbabwean national, Eldrie Ngwenya, has been arrested and appeared before the Polokwane Magistrate’s Court following a police operation that uncovered a large quantity of illicit Reming...

item.title: Philippines to Establish Military Space Unit and Acquire Satellites by 2028
item.description: The Armed Forces of the Philippines (AFP) is planning to create a space center that will eventually evolve into a space force or command, according to AFP chief General Romeo Brawner Jr. This initiati...

item.title: US-Iran conflict escalates with military strikes and regional tensions
item.description: The article details the escalating conflict between the United States and Iran, following a series of military strikes by the US on Iranian targets in response to attacks on commercial vessels in the ...

item.title: Supreme Court Rules External Agencies Can't Record Findings Against Lawyers
item.description: The Supreme Court recently issued a landmark ruling stating that external agencies or entities cannot record findings or evidence against legal professionals without proper judicial authorization. Thi...

item.title: Exploring Junk Journaling: A Creative Way to Document Life's Moments
item.description: Junk journaling is a stress-free alternative to traditional journaling that uses everyday found items and ephemera to document daily experiences. Unlike conventional methods, it allows for creativity ...

item.title: Senate probes NWDC governance issues, financial mismanagement
item.description: The Senate Committee on Regional Development probed the North West Development Commission (NWDC) over governance challenges, financial mismanagement, and delayed appointments of executive directors. L...

item.title: Navigating Financial Support for Adult Children in Australia's Economic Climate
item.description: The article explores the financial challenges faced by younger Australians in their 30s, struggling with mortgage and living costs amid rising interest rates. Older Australians in their 60s, with stab...

item.title: Scientists Develop Programmable Material to Control Heat
item.description: Researchers at Osaka Metropolitan University have created a groundbreaking material that can control and 'program' heat, enabling precise thermal radiation direction, mode switching, and memory retent...

item.title: Legal Dispute Over Property Boundaries and Neighborly Rights
item.description: The article discusses a legal dispute between neighbors regarding property boundaries and the right to paint one's side of a shared fence. A consumer lawyer, DEAN DUNHAM KC, provides advice on whether...

item.title: Blue Jays Struggle to Turn Around Season Amid Injuries and Slump
item.description: The Toronto Blue Jays are facing a challenging season, with frustrations mounting as they struggle to improve their performance. Entering a critical phase of the season, the team has lost four consecu...

item.title: California DMV identifies anomalies in 11,000 driver tests, prompting license retakes
item.description: California's Department of Motor Vehicles (DMV) has identified anomalies in the results of certain knowledge tests, prompting approximately 11,000 drivers to retake their written exams within the next...

item.title: CNN Panel Debates Harriet Tubman's Placement on $20 Bill, With Trump Supporters and Democrats Clash
item.description: A heated debate erupted on a CNN panel regarding the decision to replace Andrew Jackson's face on the $20 bill with Harriet Tubman, a historical figure known for leading enslaved people to freedom via...

item.title: Super Typhoon Inday Enters PAR with Wind Signal No. 1 in 7 Provinces
item.description: The article highlights the entry of Super Typhoon Inday into the Philippine Area of Responsibility (PAR), triggering wind signal no. 1 in seven provinces. It details the typhoon's impact, including po...

item.title: B.C. Nurses Continue Strike Action Amid Ongoing Dispute
item.description: B.C. nurses have resumed strike actions, including a picket line at Vancouver General Hospital, following the rejection of a proposed deal by two-thirds of union members. The BC Nurses’ Union began jo...
